nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Fontaine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Normand, Pierre, Emile, Fernand, Lucien, Marcel, Adelard, Henri, Jacques, Laurent.

French and Walloon: topographic name for someone living near a spring or well, from Old French fontaine, from Late Latin fontana, a derivative of Latin fons (see Font ); or a habitational name from Fontaine, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Fountaine .
Shortened form of French   Lafontaine  , a variant of 1 above.

variants.name: Fontenot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Ulysse, Andrus, Alcide, Andre, Edouard, Lucien, Marise, Monique, Remy.

French: variant of Fonteneau (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a topographic name from a diminutive of Old French fontaine ‘spring, well’ (see Fontaine ), or a habitational name from any of several places mainly in the westcentral part of France so named. The surname Fontenot is very rare in France.
History: Jean-Louis Fonteneau/Fontenot from Montierneuf in Vienne, France, married Louise-Angélique Henry in Mobile, AL, in 1726.

variants.name: Fontenelle
meanings: French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French fontaine ‘spring, well’ (see Fontaine ), or a habitational name from (La) Fontenelle, the name of several places in various parts of France, of the same origin.

variants.name: Fontenette
meanings: French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French fontaine ‘spring, well’ (see Fontaine ), or a habitational name from Fontenette, the name of several places mainly in the central part of France, of the same origin.

variants.name: Fountain
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from one or more of the places in France named Fontaine, Fonteyne, or Lafontaine (see   Fontaine  ).

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a spring or well, from Middle English fontayne (Old French fontaine) ‘spring, fountain’, sometimes with a plural -s, and synonymous with Well , of which Fountain(es) may sometimes have been a translation.

variants.name: Font
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Miguel, Rafael, Eduardo, Raul, Eugenio, Pedro, Roberto, Aida.
Catalan and southern French: topographic name for someone living near a spring or well, Catalan and Occitan font ‘spring, well’ (from Latin fons, genitive fontis).
English: variant of   Fant  .

variants.name: Fontan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Angel, Benigno, Carlos, Jose, Juana, Manuel, Pedro, Adolfo, Aida, Alfredo, Candido, Emilia.
Spanish and Galician (Fontán): habitational name from any of the many places called Fontán, from fontán ‘spring’.
French: variant of   Fontaine  .

nameMeanings.name: Cecily
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cecilie
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cicely
meanings: Variant of   Cecily .  This was a common form of the name in the Middle Ages, and in the 20th century was well known as the name of the British actress and singer  Cicely Courtneidge  (  1893–1980 ).

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Cecily de la Fontaine was born in 1095, in Yorkshire, England. She married Osbert de la Warde in 1115, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons.
